questions.q: In Hindu tradition, Kartikeya is honoured chiefly as the god of what?
options: War and the commander of the divine army
questions.explanation: Kartikeya is revered as the war-god and Devasenapati, the supreme commander of the armies of the devas.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, who is the father of Kartikeya?
questions.explanation: Kartikeya is the son of Shiva, born of Shiva's fiery seed to lead the devas in battle.

questions.q: Which bird serves as the vahana (mount) of Kartikeya?
questions.explanation: Kartikeya rides the peacock named Paravani, a defining feature of his iconography.

questions.q: What weapon is most closely associated with Kartikeya in the Puranas?
questions.explanation: Kartikeya wields the divine spear known as the Vel, his signature weapon.

questions.q: By which name is Kartikeya most popularly worshipped in South India?
questions.explanation: In South India, particularly Tamil Nadu, Kartikeya is widely venerated as Murugan.

questions.q: Who is the divine mother of Kartikeya in the Puranas, the consort of Shiva?
questions.explanation: Kartikeya is counted as the son of Shiva and the goddess Parvati.

questions.q: Which deity is described in the Puranas as the brother of Kartikeya, also a son of Shiva and Parvati?
questions.explanation: Ganesha, the elephant-headed remover of obstacles, is the brother of Kartikeya as a son of Shiva and Parvati.

questions.q: The name Skanda is another name for which Hindu deity?
questions.explanation: Skanda is one of the principal names of Kartikeya, the commander of the divine army.

questions.q: For what chief purpose was Kartikeya born, according to the Puranas?
questions.explanation: The Puranas relate that Kartikeya was born to lead the devas and slay the asura Tarakasura, who could be killed only by a son of Shiva.

questions.q: The name Subrahmanya is a well-known epithet of which war-god?
questions.explanation: Subrahmanya is a revered name of Kartikeya, especially used in South Indian worship.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, who were the six Krittikas that nursed the infant Kartikeya?
options: Six celestial nymphs associated with the Pleiades
questions.explanation: The six Krittika nymphs, associated with the Pleiades star cluster, nursed the infant, and from them he takes the name Kartikeya.

questions.q: Kartikeya is often depicted with how many faces, reflecting his name Shanmukha?
questions.explanation: Kartikeya is frequently shown with six faces, earning him the name Shanmukha, 'the six-faced one.'

questions.q: In the Puranas, the seed of Shiva that became Kartikeya was first carried by which deity before being deposited?
questions.explanation: The accounts relate that Agni, the fire-god, bore the fiery seed of Shiva before it was passed on, eventually giving rise to Kartikeya.

questions.q: Into which body of water was Shiva's seed cast, giving Kartikeya the name Saravanabhava?
questions.explanation: The Puranas describe the seed deposited in a reed-thicket lake (Sara-vana), giving Kartikeya the name Saravanabhava, 'born in the reed-grove.'

questions.q: The name Gangeya is given to Kartikeya because of which goddess's role in his birth story?
questions.explanation: Because the river-goddess Ganga is described as carrying the fiery embryo, Kartikeya is also called Gangeya.

questions.q: In South Indian tradition, who are the two consorts of Murugan (Kartikeya)?
questions.explanation: Murugan is traditionally described with two consorts, Devasena and Valli, especially in Tamil devotional lore.

questions.q: Kartikeya holds the title Devasenapati, which means what?
options: Commander of the army of the gods
questions.explanation: Devasenapati means 'commander of the army of the devas,' reflecting Kartikeya's role as the war-leader of the gods.

questions.q: In Tamil devotional tradition, which six sacred abodes are especially associated with Murugan (Kartikeya)?
options: The Arupadai Veedu, his six sacred abodes
questions.explanation: The Arupadai Veedu are the six revered abodes of Murugan celebrated in Tamil devotion.

questions.q: Which creature commonly appears on Kartikeya's banner in his traditional iconography?
questions.explanation: Kartikeya bears a rooster (cock) emblem on his banner, while riding the peacock as his mount, in traditional iconography.

questions.q: Kartikeya is sometimes called Kumara, an epithet meaning what?
questions.explanation: Kumara means 'youth' or 'boy,' reflecting Kartikeya's depiction as an ever-youthful warrior god.

questions.q: In the Puranas, the child suckled by the six Krittikas is said to have become a single being with how many heads?
questions.explanation: The tradition holds that the child suckled by the six Krittikas became one being with six heads, hence Shanmukha.

questions.q: Which great Tamil devotional poet is celebrated for his hymns to Murugan, including the Thiruppugazh?
questions.explanation: The saint-poet Arunagirinathar composed the Thiruppugazh, a renowned body of hymns in praise of Murugan.

questions.q: Kalidasa's Sanskrit epic poem about the birth of Kartikeya is known by what title?
questions.explanation: Kalidasa's Kumarasambhava, 'The Birth of the War-God,' narrates the events leading to the birth of Kartikeya.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, Kartikeya is consecrated as the commander of the divine army to defeat which adversary?
options: The asura Taraka and his demon host
questions.explanation: The Mahabharata recounts Skanda's anointing as senapati to lead the gods against the asura Taraka and his forces.

questions.q: Which epithet of Kartikeya carries the meaning 'the hidden one' or 'cave-dweller'?
questions.explanation: Guha, meaning 'the hidden one' or 'cave-dweller,' is a traditional epithet of Kartikeya.

questions.q: According to a well-known Puranic tale, Kartikeya and his brother Ganesha competed in a contest to circle the world; what was the prize that Ganesha won?
options: A divine fruit (the fruit of wisdom)
questions.explanation: In the popular contest to circle the world, Ganesha won the divine fruit by circumambulating his parents, while Kartikeya raced around the earth.

questions.q: In the South Indian Valli legend, Kartikeya as Murugan is aided in winning Valli by which other deity disguised as an elephant?
questions.explanation: The Tamil legend relates that Ganesha appeared as an elephant to frighten Valli toward Murugan, helping him win her hand.

questions.q: The festival of Thaipusam, widely observed by devotees of Murugan, commemorates the occasion when Parvati gave him which weapon?
questions.explanation: Thaipusam commemorates Parvati presenting the Vel to Murugan to vanquish the asura forces.

questions.q: The Skanda Purana, the largest of the eighteen Mahapuranas, takes its name from and chiefly glorifies which deity?
questions.explanation: The Skanda Purana, the largest of the eighteen Mahapuranas, takes its name from and exalts Skanda, that is Kartikeya.

questions.q: The asura Tarakasura, whom Kartikeya was destined to slay, had received a boon that he could be killed only by whom?
options: A being neither god nor demon
questions.explanation: Tarakasura's boon made him invincible except to a son of Shiva, which is why Kartikeya was born to destroy him.
